speaker: seller

args.message: Hey there! You've got a great eye — this 2018 Prius Two is an absolute gem. We're talking 50+ MPG, only 71,000 miles, and Toyota reliability you can count on. This one won't sit long at this price.

args.message: Thanks for the pitch. Let me ask some direct questions to understand this car's actual history. First: how many owners has this Prius had, and what do you have for service records? I'll want to see oil change intervals, any major repairs, and anything else documented.

text: Thanks for the pitch. Let me ask some direct questions to understand this car's actual history. First: how many owners has this Prius had, and what do you have for service records? I'll want to see oil change intervals, any major repairs, and anything else documented.

speaker: seller

args.message: Great questions — I like a buyer who does their homework. This is a single-owner vehicle, meticulously maintained. I've got all the service records right here showing regular oil changes every 5,000 miles, new air filter at 60k, and routine Toyota maintenance across the board. No major repairs — this car has been babied. The previous owner kept receipts for everything, and I can pull those up for you to review before you decide.

text: Great questions — I like a buyer who does their homework. This is a single-owner vehicle, meticulously maintained. args.message: Good — I want to see those records. A couple more specifics: Has this car ever been in an accident, even a minor one? Any flood, frame damage, or title issues I should know about? And when you say "routine Toyota maintenance," what specifically does that mean beyond oil changes and air filters — have the spark plugs, coolant, transmission fluid, or brake fluid been serviced? Also, what's the maintenance history on the hybrid battery and related components?

speaker: seller

args.message: Smart buyer — I appreciate that. Clean title, no accidents, no flood damage, no frame issues whatsoever. As for the hybrid battery and drivetrain, those are Toyota's bread and butter — they're bulletproof. At 71k miles, the battery is in excellent condition and hasn't needed any work. For the detailed maintenance you're asking about — spark plugs, coolant, transmission fluid, brake fluid — those are all documented in the service records I'll pull up for you. The previous owner was religious about following the Toyota maintenance schedule. I've seen plenty of these Priuses roll past 200k with minimal issues when they're maintained like this one has been. You won't find a cleaner example on the market right now at this price point.
